description:
Capacity group definition.

A capacity group represents machines with identical hardware profiles.
Each group becomes a separate Auto Scaling Group (AWS), Managed Instance Group (GCP),
or VM Scale Set (Azure).

groupId.description: Unique identifier for this capacity group (must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens)

instanceType.description:
Provider machine selected at deployment time.
`alien.ts` should declare portable requirements; preflight materialization
fills this field from `StackSettings.compute`.

minSize.description: Minimum number of machines (can be 0 for scale-to-zero)

nestedVirtualization.description:
Require instance types that expose nested virtualization (VT-x/EPT)
to guest VMs. This is needed by workloads that boot nested VMs inside
containers.
When true, the controller's instance-type selector is constrained
to a vetted nested-virt-capable allowlist.

oneOf.description: Machine resource profile (auto-derived from instance_type if not specified)
CPU architecture required or provided by this machine profile.

cpu.description:
CPU cores per machine (hardware total) - stored as string to preserve precision
(e.g., "8.0", "4.5")

oneOf.description:
Source-declared scale policy and installer-editable bounds.

Older stacks only have `minSize` and `maxSize`; planners derive an exact
fixed/autoscale policy from those selected bounds when this field is absent.
A fixed-size pool where the installer can choose the fixed machine count.

default.description: Default value recommended when no installer override is supplied.

oneOf.description: An autoscaling pool with separately bounded min and max counts.

default.description: Default value recommended when no installer override is supplied.
Default value recommended when no installer override is supplied.
